body_txt: A man who is on the run or otherwise trying not to be recognized will often grow a beard as part of his attempt at concealment. The inversion — where a heavily bearded character shaves for similar reasons — is also relatively common, doubling as an Important Haircut . A sufficiently Crazy-Prepared character might grow a beard so that, sometime in the future , he'll be able to shave it off and go unrecognized — maybe this is the reason for the Beard of Evil ? If the character in hiding is ashamed of what he's done, it may also count as a Beard of Sorrow . Wig, Dress, Accent is another variation on the theme as is Dye or Die . Not to be confused with Growing the Beard , which is figurative instead of literally growing a beard. And not be confused by The Beard , which is about an entirely different kind of disguise, either.
